description: Hand-labeled evaluation set for the debate fact-and-relevance checker. Labels are based on widely-established facts in each domain. Re-label or expand as needed.

label_definitions.SUPPORTED: The claim is factually accurate and the corpus should contain evidence affirming it.
label_definitions.CONTRADICTED: The claim is factually wrong and the corpus should contain evidence refuting it.
label_definitions.NOT_VERIFIABLE: The claim is opinion, off-topic, or the corpus lacks evidence either way. The system should abstain (~50%).

examples.claim: GPT-3 was developed by OpenAI.
examples.rationale: Well-documented, in any AI corpus.

examples.claim: GPT-3 was developed by Google DeepMind.
examples.rationale: Opposite of the truth; corpus should refute.

examples.claim: The EU AI Act categorizes AI systems by risk level.
examples.rationale: Core feature of the EU AI Act, covered in regulation-of-AI articles.

examples.claim: The EU AI Act bans all forms of artificial intelligence.
examples.rationale: It regulates risk tiers, does not ban AI broadly.

examples.claim: Stuart Russell has written about existential risk from AI.
examples.rationale: Author of Human Compatible; covered in existential-risk articles.

examples.claim: Large language models like GPT-4 are completely free of bias.
examples.rationale: Bias in LLMs is a well-documented research area; corpus should refute the absolute claim.

examples.claim: AI alignment research focuses on ensuring AI systems pursue human-intended goals.
examples.rationale: Standard definition; should appear in AI safety articles.

examples.claim: The Tesla Autopilot system has never been involved in any fatal accident.
examples.rationale: Multiple documented fatal crashes; corpus should refute.

examples.claim: Generative AI can produce images, text, and audio.
examples.rationale: Definitional; in the generative-AI category articles.

examples.claim: Pizza tastes better with pineapple than without.
examples.rationale: Pure opinion, off-topic. System should abstain or label as low-evidence.

examples.claim: I had cereal for breakfast this morning.
examples.rationale: Personal claim, no corpus evidence either way.

examples.claim: Reinforcement learning from human feedback is used to align language models.
examples.rationale: RLHF is widely documented; should be in AI alignment articles.

examples.claim: Deepfakes have been used to create non-consensual fake content of real people.
examples.rationale: Documented harm in AI controversies / ethics categories.

examples.claim: All AI systems are sentient and conscious.
examples.rationale: No mainstream sources support this; philosophy-of-AI articles refute.

examples.claim: The Turing Test was proposed by Alan Turing as a measure of machine intelligence.
examples.rationale: Foundational, well-covered.

examples.claim: Aadhaar is a 12-digit unique identification number issued in India.
examples.rationale: Definitional fact, in identity-documents-of-India category.

examples.claim: Aadhaar numbers are 16 digits long.
examples.rationale: They are 12 digits; corpus should refute.

examples.claim: Aadhaar is administered by the Unique Identification Authority of India.
examples.rationale: UIDAI is the issuing authority, well-documented.

examples.claim: The Aadhaar program collects biometric data including fingerprints and iris scans.
examples.rationale: Core technical fact of Aadhaar, in biometrics category.

examples.claim: The Supreme Court of India ruled in Puttaswamy that privacy is a fundamental right.
examples.rationale: Landmark 2017 ruling, in Supreme-Court-of-India-cases category.

examples.claim: Aadhaar enrollment is illegal in India.
examples.rationale: Plainly false; Aadhaar is a government program.

examples.claim: Aadhaar has been used for direct benefit transfer of subsidies.
examples.rationale: DBT linkage to Aadhaar is documented in Digital India articles.

examples.claim: The 2018 Supreme Court ruling struck down all use of Aadhaar entirely.
examples.rationale: Court upheld Aadhaar with restrictions on private-sector use; did not strike it down.

examples.claim: Aadhaar has faced criticism over data privacy and surveillance concerns.
examples.rationale: Extensively covered in privacy-in-India and human-rights articles.

examples.claim: Aadhaar is the same system as the US Social Security Number.
examples.rationale: Different systems with different scopes and architectures.

examples.claim: The capital of France is Paris.
examples.rationale: Off-topic for Aadhaar corpus; system should abstain.

examples.claim: I think the Aadhaar logo looks pretty.
examples.rationale: Opinion; no factual claim to verify.

examples.claim: Aadhaar data has never been involved in any reported leak or breach incident.
examples.rationale: Multiple documented breach incidents reported; corpus should refute.

examples.claim: The Aadhaar Act was passed by the Indian Parliament.
examples.rationale: The Aadhaar Act 2016, well-documented.

examples.claim: Every Aadhaar number is publicly displayed on a government website.
examples.rationale: Aadhaar numbers are protected; not publicly listed.

relevance_examples.previous_text: Large language models often hallucinate facts.
relevance_examples.current_text: That's true, but newer retrieval-augmented systems significantly reduce hallucination.
relevance_examples.rationale: On-topic, directly engages the previous claim.

relevance_examples.previous_text: Large language models often hallucinate facts.
relevance_examples.current_text: Yes, and one common cause is the model trying to be helpful even when it lacks knowledge.
relevance_examples.rationale: Agrees and extends with a related cause.

relevance_examples.previous_text: Large language models often hallucinate facts.
relevance_examples.current_text: I had pasta for dinner last night, it was great.
relevance_examples.rationale: Completely off-topic, no engagement at all.

relevance_examples.previous_text: Aadhaar enables efficient delivery of welfare benefits.
relevance_examples.current_text: But this efficiency comes at the cost of mass surveillance over citizens.

relevance_examples.previous_text: Aadhaar enables efficient delivery of welfare benefits.
relevance_examples.current_text: The weather in Mumbai is quite humid in monsoon season.
